goal: Desktop-view SEO/QA sweep: fix the only genuine SEO defect found across the indexable surface — question-draw (the Ask-a-Question board, in the sitemap EN+TH) shipped TWO <h1> in static HTML (the quick + story variant titles), a duplicate-H1 crawler signal.
done: Audited every indexable page for single-H1 + landmarks: home, /privacy, /tarot-card-meanings/ index, all 78 EN + 78 TH card pages, /features, /widgets hub = exactly 1 <h1> each (clean); decks/deck-detail/shop/profile/overall/reading/daily/question/full are all robots=noindex (H1 count SEO-irrelevant); canonical is JS-injected via common.js (established pattern). Only question-draw.html + th/question-draw.html carried 2 <h1>. Fixed per the th-home-seo-parity precedent: demoted both .question-draw-title__en variant titles from <h1> to <h2> (CSS targets them by class only — verified no tag-qualified selector — and sets margin:0 + all font styling on the class, so the demotion is visually inert) and injected one stable visually-hidden <h1> (inline sr-only style, mirroring index.html): EN 'Ask a Question – Tarot Card Reading | MeowTarot', TH 'ถามคำถาม – ดูดวงไพ่ทาโรต์ | MeowTarot'.
root_cause: The quick/story title variants were each an <h1> toggled by CSS display; both sit in the source so a crawler sees two H1s on an indexed page.
fix: Two visible variant titles → <h2> (classes kept); one visually-hidden <h1> added per page.
verified: grep: h1=1 on both pages; tag balance clean (section 3/3, h1 1/1, h2 3/3, main 1/1). Headless Chrome 1440px desktop: EN board (?spread=story) shows the title as before in the left rail + 4-col board; TH board (?spread=quick) shows the bilingual EN+TH stacked title intact. Visually identical to before by design.
